source.title: Inhibitory threshold for critical-period activation in primary visual cortex.

confidence_rationale: Causal, not correlational: GAD65-knockout mice never open the critical period spontaneously, yet a benzodiazepine restores plasticity at any age, and early GABA enhancement permanently closes it. This establishes that the plasticity window is gated by a controllable inhibitory (E/I) threshold and is triggered 'once in life' -- precisely the time-bounded plasticity asymmetry INV-074 requires. High confidence; discounted only for single-system biology and the absolute-irreversibility nuance.
failure_signatures: Early enhancement of GABA transmission rendered animals permanently insensitive to monocular deprivation as adults -- the window can be spent prematurely; INV-074's diversity circuits could be foreclosed by an over-early closure trigger, not only by a missed opening.

Plasticity 'may otherwise lie dormant' indefinitely without the threshold being reached -- a system that never crosses the E/I trigger never gets a window at all, which is a stronger (and more fragile) reading than 'the window is merely brief'.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The potential for ocular-dominance plasticity is retained throughout life until an intracortical inhibitory threshold is attained; GAD65-knockout mice never trigger their critical period but benzodiazepines unmask plasticity at any age, while early GABA enhancement permanently prevents it -- a threshold level of inhibition triggers, once in life, an experience-dependent consolidation window.
mapping.ree_translation: Fagiolini & Hensch supply the causal gate INV-074 presupposes: the plasticity window is not a passive maturational drift but an actively triggered, threshold-crossing event that opens 'once in life'. In REE terms this is the licence for MECH-333's bounded open phase to be a discrete, parameterised trigger rather than an emergent side effect of training time.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The trigger here is a specific neurochemical variable (intracortical GABA/E-I balance); REE has no GABA, so the transfer is to the abstract structure 'a controllable scalar threshold opens a one-shot plasticity window', not to the molecular mechanism. Evidences INV-074's gating logic, and motivates MECH-333, but does not by itself speak to closure (MECH-334).
mapping.source_context: Mice (GAD65 knockout and wild-type); benzodiazepine/diazepam pharmacology; monocular deprivation; visual cortex plasticity readout.

confidence_components.notes: Nature, Hensch -- definitive causal critical-period gating paper. Mapping fidelity high for the 'actively triggered one-shot window' structure that underwrites INV-074/MECH-333. Transfer risk moderate: the abstract gating structure transfers cleanly even though the GABA substrate does not.
